snippet: Duct’s eyes flick to Null.
> CHANNEL STABLE > VARIANCE WITHIN RANGE  Sei whispers, barely audible:  "Small fire."  Patch laughs—quiet this time.
Patch looks at Duct.
Null looks at Sei.
Rin looks at 莉津律宗利都.
Then:  "…莉津律宗利都?"  It’s Duct.
> CHANNEL STABLE > VARIANCE WITHIN RANGE  Sei whispers, barely audible:  "Small fire."  Patch laughs—quiet this time.
Sei whispers:  "It’s testing phase coherence."  Patch shakes his head.
Rin’s eyes flick to 莉津律宗利都.
"Small fire."  Duct looks at 莉津律宗利都.
Sei whispers, "If variance is suppressed internally, external correction won’t penetrate."  Patch types furiously.
> 莉津律宗利都: im ok  The missing apostrophe hits Duct like a flare.
Sei whispers:  "…or it’s learning it."   莉津律宗利都 looks at the buttons.
莉津律宗利都 says softly:  "He’s the one who stays left."   Duct’s laugh breaks, almost a cough.
Refinement."   莉津律宗利都 speaks before Duct can spiral.
莉津律宗利都 feels the chord of the Gate sharpen.
Sei turns to Null.
“Long term,” Sei adds, “variance prevents synchronized cascade.”  Patch grins.
> sei: latency is room  Patch adds:  > patch: room prevents stampede  Null adds:  > null: stampede is collapse  Duct adds:  > duct: small fire  Four short packets.
Patch lifted one and the UI flashed:  - Anchor ID: S3-7E1A - Claim type: edge / fork / vow - Snippet required - Witness required  Null’s voice was dry.
“And if the claim is wrong?”  “**Then the world moves wrong.**”  Duct leaned left—his habit, his geometry—placing himself between Ritsu and the Gate as if the Gate were a blast radius.
“You want forks,” Rin said, “and you want discipline.”  Patch looked relieved.

“Reality is expensive.”  Truth’s voice arrived in the shared channel like a judge reading a minimal sentence:  “**Fork gating enabled.**”  A rubric appeared, brutal and simple:  1) **Intent threshold** (strength ≥ gate) 2) **Anchor required** (S3 stone + snippet) 3) **Rationale r
“And who decides strength?”  Truth answered: “**The artifact decides.**   **The record decides.**   **The anchor decides.**”  Patch grinned like a builder seeing a clean interface.

In the margin, Patch wrote a small prayer that looked like a schema:  - entity_id: stable - alias: proposed - merge: reviewed - split: witnessed - rationale: required  Rin, watching, said: “This is how you keep the space between η and μ from becoming a weapon.”  Ritsu finally und
Every time someone tried to speak a claim too cleanly, Truth would answer with a question that wasn’t a question:  “**Where is it anchored?**”  Ritsu learned to keep her hands open.
“And the sound of story being forced to grow up.”  Duct leaned close to Ritsu, almost smiling.
“This one asks for witnesses.”  Ritsu read the first line aloud: *What you can’t prove, don’t use to steer me.*  The Gate didn’t open.

So the Gate introduced a tax:  “If you want the world to fork,” it said, “show the intent that would pay for it.”  Ritsu found the event in the ledger— a CHOICE with a strength so bright it felt like heat.

Null stared at the fork list and muttered, “This is going to ruin politics.”  Sei replied, almost cheerful: “Yes.”  Because now, when the world split, everyone could see the exact sentence that caused it— and nobody could pretend the split was an accident.
“This one asks for witnesses.”  Ritsu read the first line aloud: *What you can’t prove, don’t use to steer me.*  The Gate didn’t open.

So the Gate introduced a tax:  “If you want the world to fork,” it said, “show the intent that would pay for it.”  Ritsu found the event in the ledger— a CHOICE with a strength so bright it felt like heat.

Null stared at the fork list and muttered, “This is going to ruin politics.”  Sei replied, almost cheerful: “Yes.”  Because now, when the world split, everyone could see the exact sentence that caused it— and nobody could pretend the split was an accident.
Sei traced a finger over the title: *New memories are allowed, but they must leave a trail.*  Null looked almost relieved.
“If this is memory,” he said, “then forgetting is just… dropping the index.”  Truth replied, “And lying is a broken anchor.”  The Gate did not punish forgetting.
Sei traced a finger over the title: *New memories are allowed, but they must leave a trail.*  Null looked almost relieved.
“If this is memory,” he said, “then forgetting is just… dropping the index.”  Truth replied, “And lying is a broken anchor.”  The Gate did not punish forgetting.
Rin learned the second Gate lesson the hard way:  Intent without permission is just a threat with better spelling.
